title: Deterioration of stone materials in the Angkor monuments, Cambodia
highlights: Angkor monuments in Cambodia are built mainly of sandstone and laterite. This paper mainly describes the  ...    deterioration and the need for restoration is urgent. The restoration of the Angkor monuments was initiated by the EFEO  ...    The Angkor monuments are mainly made of sandstone and laterite. Brick is also used in sanctuaries of the relatively old monuments such as Bakong, Lolei, Prasat Kravan, Baksei Chamkrong, East Mebon and Pre Rup. Laterite tends to be used in enclosing walls, platforms and pavements. No laterite is used in sanctuaries except for Prasat Suor Prat. Sandstone is  ...    The compressive strength, orientation of bedding plane, constituent minerals, porosity and bioactivity can be listed up  ...    Deterioration is frequently observed in the grey to yellowish brown sandstone, but not in the red sandstone and the greenish greywacke of the Angkor monuments. title: La reprise des travaux de la Terrasse du Roi lépreux - Persée
highlights: L'École Française d'Extrême-Orient s'est engagée dans la reprise des travaux de restauration sur le site d'Angkor.
